ModuleName: 2 - Control Flow and Python Data Structures

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: An outline of all topics covered in the module, including conditional statements (if/elif/else), for and while loops, loop control with break and continue, and Python data structures such as lists, serving as a navigation guide for learners and a planning reference for instructors.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive reading module covering Python control flow with if, elif, and else conditional statements, explaining Boolean evaluation and branching logic; learners read through explanations and examples to understand how programs make decisions.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive reading module covering Python control flow with if, elif, and else conditional statements, explaining Boolean evaluation and branching logic; learners read through explanations and examples to understand how programs make decisions.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive reading module introducing Python lists as a core data structure, covering how to create, access, and manipulate ordered collections using square-bracket syntax; learners read through explanations and examples to build foundational knowledge of list operations.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (10 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 10-question quiz bank assessing learners' understanding of conditional statements, the elif clause, the range() function, loop selection, and break behavior in nested loops, used to test comprehension of core control flow concepts.

Artifacts.Name: Drag-Drop Code Use Match
Artifacts.Description: An interactive drag-and-drop activity covering nested conditionals in which learners match 8 code snippets or keywords to their correct descriptions, with correct answers locking in place and incorrect ones bouncing back, used to reinforce understanding of nested conditional syntax and logic.

Artifacts.Name: Drag-Drop Data Structure Match
Artifacts.Description: An interactive drag-and-drop activity in which learners match Python data structure names (e.g., list, tuple, dictionary) to 8 engineering scenarios that best illustrate their use, used to practice selecting the appropriate data structure for real-world engineering contexts.
